title: The 7 Best Herbal Essential Oils for Calming Itchy Skin

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, and also has antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the skin, making it feel cooler and more comfortable.

Kaempferol is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and swelling, and also soothe and calm irritated skin, providing relief from itching and discomfort.
Saponins are good for itchy skin because they have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis essential oil is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can reduce swelling and redness that often come with itchy skin. It also has antiseptic properties, which help to prevent infections that can make itchy skin worse. Calendula oil is rich in antioxidants, which protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. These free radicals can make itchy skin feel worse and take longer to heal. Calendula oil also has soothing and cooling effects, which can help to calm itchy skin and provide relief. When applied topically, calendula oil can help to reduce itching and inflammation, making it a popular natural remedy for itchy skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Chamomilla recutita herbal essential oil, start by gathering dried flowers of the plant, also known as German chamomile. You can find these at a local health food store or online. Next, place the dried flowers in a clean glass jar and cover them with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil. The general ratio is one part flowers to three parts oil. Stir the mixture well and seal the jar. Store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process is called maceration, and it helps to extract the beneficial properties from the flowers into the oil. After the maceration period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. The resulting oil can be used topically to help soothe itchy skin, promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Aloin is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing the itching and discomfort.
Anthraquinones are good for itchy skin because they have anti-inflammatory and antipruritic properties that help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing the urge to scratch and promoting healing.
Aloe-emodin has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce itching and soothe itchy skin by calming the skin's natural response to irritation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oil, also known as aloe vera oil, is a popular remedy for itchy skin. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness, which can make itchy skin feel even worse. When applied to the skin, aloe vera oil creates a soothing barrier that calms the skin and relieves itching. This is because it contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have powerful anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Aloe vera oil also helps to lock in moisture, which is essential for keeping the skin hydrated and healthy. Additionally, it has antioxidant properties that protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to itchiness and other skin problems.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Chamomilla recutita herbal essential oil, you will need fresh or dried flowers of the plant, a clean glass container, a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil, and a dark glass bottle to store the oil in. First, start by drying the flowers in a cool, dry place or by placing them in a low-temperature oven for a few hours to remove excess moisture. Once the flowers are dry, add them to the glass container and pour in the carrier oil, making sure that the flowers are completely covered. Let the mixture sit for 2-4 weeks, shaking the container gently every few days, to allow the oil to infuse into the carrier oil. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, and discard the dried flowers. The resulting oil can be used to help soothe itchy skin, and can be applied topically in small amounts after diluting it with a carrier oil.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal essential oils for itchy skin

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties that help reduce swelling, redness, and irritation, providing relief from itchy and uncomfortable skin.
Tannins are good for itchy skin because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling, soothing the skin and providing relief from itching and discomfort.
Isorhapontigenin is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and irritation, providing relief and soothing the skin.
Polyphenols are good for itchy skin because they have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help calm down irritated skin and reduce itching and redness.
Kaempferol is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and itching, allowing the skin to heal and feel relieved.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica essential oil, also known as nettle oil, is often used to soothe itchy skin. This is because it has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help reduce redness and swelling. When applied topically, the oil can calm the skin's itchiness and discomfort. It also has antiseptic properties that prevent infections from developing, which can make itchy skin even worse. Furthermore, nettle oil is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. This can lead to long-term benefits, such as softer and healthier-looking skin. The oil can also be used to reduce itching caused by allergic reactions and skin irritations.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Chamomilla recutita herbal essential oil for itchy skin, start by drying a large quantity of fresh Chamomilla recutita flowers, also known as German chamomile. You can dry them by spreading them out in a single layer on a paper bag or a clean cloth, allowing them to air dry in a cool, dark place for 1-2 weeks. Once the flowers are dry and crumbly, grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle. Next, combine 1 cup of the dried flower powder with 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar gently every day to help the oil extract the beneficial properties from the dried flowers. After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and leaving the oil behind. The resulting herbal essential oil can be used topically to soothe itchy skin, but be sure to do a patch test first to ensure you don't have any sensitivity to the oil.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal essential oils for itchy skin

Linalool helps to soothe itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling, and also has antipruritic properties that calm the itching sensation.
Quercetin is good for itchy skin because it reduces inflammation, fights free radicals, and soothes the skin, all of which help to calm and relieve itchy skin irritations.
Catechin is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching, making it a soothing and calming ingredient for irritated skin.

Terpinen-4-ol is good for itchy skin because it has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce redness and soothe irritated skin, making it a natural remedy for relieving itchiness and discomfort.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil, also known as tea tree oil, is good for itchy skin because of its powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. When applied to itchy skin, it helps to reduce redness and swelling by preventing the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can irritate the skin. The oil's antiseptic properties also help to prevent infections that can cause itching and discomfort. Additionally, tea tree oil has a cooling effect that can provide relief from itchy and irritated skin. Its anti-inflammatory properties can also soothe and calm the skin, reducing the urge to scratch and further irritate the skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Chamomilla recutita herbal essential oil, start by harvesting or purchasing dried flowers of the plant, also known as German chamomile. Next, dry the flowers thoroughly, either by air-drying them or by placing them in a low-temperature oven (around 150-200°F) for a few hours. Once the flowers are completely dry, place them in a clean glass jar with a lid, making sure to fill the jar about 2/3 full. Now, cover the flowers with a carrier oil, such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, making sure the flowers are completely submerged. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. After the steeping process, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Finally, transfer the herbal essential oil to a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid, and store it in a cool, dark place to preserve its medicinal properties. This herbal essential oil can be used to soothe itchy skin, promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oil is a natural remedy for itchy skin. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling. This oil is rich in antioxidants, which fight off free radicals that can cause irritation and itching. The calming effect of lavender oil can also soothe the skin, promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort. It is often used in aromatherapy to calm the mind and body, which can help alleviate stress-related skin issues. Additionally, lavender oil has antiseptic properties that can help prevent infection and promote healthy skin healing. When applied topically, it can help to reduce itching and scratching, allowing the skin to heal faster.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Chamomilla recutita herbal essential oils that help with itchy skin, start by harvesting fresh chamomile flowers and drying them thoroughly to remove excess moisture. You'll need to use a clean and sterile environment to prevent contamination. Next, fill a clean glass jar about 2/3 full with the dried chamomile flowers. Then, fill the jar with a carrier oil like jojoba or sweet almond oil, making sure the flowers are completely covered. Seal the jar and place it in a cool, dark place for about 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar gently every day. This process, called maceration, allows the chamomile's active ingredients to infuse into the carrier oil. After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting liquid is your chamomile herbal essential oil, which can be used to soothe itchy skin by applying it topically after diluting it with a carrier oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum essential oil, also known as St. John's Wort oil, is a natural remedy for itchy skin. It contains compounds like hyperforin and hypericin that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties help reduce swelling and soothe itchy skin, making it feel more comfortable. The antiseptic properties also help prevent infection, which can cause more itching and discomfort. The oil is easily absorbed into the skin, allowing it to penetrate deep and provide relief from itchy skin caused by conditions like eczema, dermatitis, and bug bites. It's also known to reduce redness and promote wound healing.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Chamomilla recutita herbal essential oil that helps with itchy skin, you'll need dried flowers of the plant, a still or distillation apparatus, and a clean glass container. First, prepare your still by filling the water reservoir and heating it up. Next, place the dried Chamomilla recutita flowers in a metal basket or a still head, making sure they're not packed too tightly. As the steam from the still passes through the flowers, it extracts their oils and carries them into a condenser, which cools the steam back into a liquid. This liquid, now containing the essential oil, is then collected in the glass container. To get the best results, be sure to use high-quality flowers and a well-maintained still, as the quality of the essential oil depends on these factors. Once you've collected the oil, strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any impurities, and store it in a dark glass bottle, out of direct sunlight.

remedies.remedy_desc: Chamomilla recutita essential oil, also known as German chamomile oil, is often used to soothe itchy skin. It contains a compound called azulene, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically, azulene can reduce redness and swelling in itchy skin, making it feel calmer and more comfortable. Chamomilla recutita oil also has a cooling effect, which can help to ease the sensation of itching and burning. Additionally, it has antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent infection in broken skin. The oil's ability to reduce inflammation and prevent infection makes it a popular natural remedy for itchy skin caused by bug bites, rashes, or dry skin. When applied regularly, Chamomilla recutita oil can help to calm and soothe itchy skin, providing relief from discomfort and promoting healthy skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Chamomilla recutita herbal essential oil that helps with itchy skin, start by harvesting fresh Chamomilla recutita flowers during the morning when they are at their most potent. Next, dry the flowers completely to remove any excess moisture by spreading them out in a single layer on a paper bag or a clean cloth, allowing them to air dry in a warm, shaded area. Once the flowers are completely dry, store them in an airtight container to preserve their potency. To extract the essential oil, heat the dried flowers in a carrier oil, such as jojoba or sweet almond oil, in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over a pot of simmering water. The oil and flowers should be heated gently for about an hour, or until the mixture has cooled to room temperature. After straining the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the oil from the solids, discard the dried flowers and transfer the oil to a clean glass bottle. Store the finished Chamomilla recutita herbal essential oil in a cool, dark place to preserve its therapeutic properties and use it topically to soothe and calm itchy skin.
remedies.image_alt: Chamomilla recutita herbal essential oils for itchy skin

intro_desc: Itchy skin can be very uncomfortable and affect your daily life. When you have itchy skin, you might feel like scratching, but scratching can make it worse and even lead to infections. That's why it's essential to find a natural way to soothe itchy skin. Herbal essential oils, especially those made from 'Calendula officinalis' and 'Aloe barbadensis', can be very helpful. 'Calendula officinalis', also known as marigold, has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and itching. It also has antiseptic properties that can help prevent infections. 'Aloe barbadensis', or aloe vera, has soothing and moisturizing properties that can calm itchy skin and keep it hydrated. When you use these essential oils, you can apply them topically to the affected area, and they can help relieve itching and discomfort. By using herbal essential oils, you can find a natural solution to itchy skin and improve your life by reducing discomfort, preventing infections, and feeling more confident and comfortable in your own skin.
supplementary_best: A combination of Calendula officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, and Urtica dioica essential oils can help remedy itchy skin. Calendula officinalis oil has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and soothe irritation. Aloe barbadensis oil is rich in aloe vera gel, which cools and moisturizes the skin, providing instant relief from itching. Urtica dioica oil, also known as nettle oil, contains compounds that reduce itching and inflammation, making it an effective anti-itch agent. This combination is effective because it addresses multiple aspects of itchy skin: reducing inflammation, cooling the skin, and providing moisturization. When used together, these oils work synergistically to provide fast and long-lasting relief from itchy skin.
supplementary_avoid: If you have itchy skin, there are some herbal essential oils you should avoid. Peppermint oil (Mentha piperita) is one to be careful with. Its cooling sensation may provide temporary relief, but it can also irritate the skin further and make itching worse. Another oil to watch out for is Eucalyptus oil (Eucalyptus globulus). It has a decongestant property that can help with respiratory issues, but it can also cause skin irritation and make itching more uncomfortable. Tea tree oil (Cananga odorata) is also not recommended for itchy skin. Although it has antiseptic properties, it can be too harsh on sensitive skin and make itching worse. Ginger oil (Zingiber officinale) may also be too intense for itchy skin. Its spicy and warming properties can cause further irritation and discomfort.
supplementary_other_remedies: Oatmeal is a popular natural remedy for itchy skin because it's soothing and gentle. When you add oatmeal to your bath water, the colloids in the oatmeal help to relieve itching and reduce inflammation. This is because oatmeal has anti-inflammatory properties that calm the skin and prevent irritation. You can also use colloidal oatmeal, which is finely ground oatmeal that's easy to dissolve in water.  Another natural remedy for itchy skin is aloe vera gel. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce redness and itching. The gel also has soothing properties that can calm the skin and promote healing. When you apply aloe vera gel to itchy skin, it forms a protective barrier that prevents further irritation and allows the skin to heal naturally. Aloe vera is also rich in vitamins and minerals that help to nourish the skin and promote healthy skin cells.  Apple cider vinegar is another natural remedy for itchy skin. The acidity in apple cider vinegar helps to balance the pH of the skin, which can become imbalanced due to irritation or inflammation. When you apply apple cider vinegar to itchy skin, it helps to reduce itching and inflammation, and promotes healing. Apple cider vinegar also has antibacterial properties that can help to prevent infection and promote healthy skin.  Baking soda is a natural antihistamine that can help to relieve itching and reduce inflammation. When you apply baking soda to itchy skin, it helps to neutralize the pH of the skin and reduce itching. Baking soda also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm the skin and promote healing. You can mix baking soda with water to create a paste that you can apply to itchy skin.  Coconut oil is a natural moisturizer that can help to relieve itchy skin. Coconut oil is rich in fatty acids that help to nourish the skin and promote healthy skin cells. When you apply coconut oil to itchy skin, it forms a protective barrier that prevents further irritation and allows the skin to heal naturally. Coconut oil also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and itching.  Epsom salt is a natural remedy for itchy skin that's rich in magnesium. Magnesium is an essential mineral that helps to relax the skin and promote healing. When you add Epsom salt to your bath water, the magnesium helps to relieve itching and reduce inflammation. Epsom salt also helps to reduce stress and promote relaxation, which can help to reduce itching and promote healing.  Tea tree oil is a natural antiseptic that can help to relieve itchy skin. Tea tree oil has antibacterial properties that can help to prevent infection and promote healthy skin. When you apply tea tree oil to itchy skin, it helps to reduce itching and inflammation, and promotes healing. However, tea tree oil can be irritating to some skin types, so it's best to use it in moderation and mix it with a carrier oil like coconut oil.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with itchy skin often experience other health issues. Dry skin (xerosis) is common, causing discomfort and cracking. Aloe vera gel soothes and moisturizes, reducing dryness.  Eczema (atopic dermatitis) is a condition where itchy skin becomes inflamed. Turmeric's active compound, curcumin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the skin.  Insect bites and allergies can cause intense itching. Oatmeal has anti-itching properties that can provide relief.  Skin conditions like psoriasis, which causes red, scaly patches, can also be itchy. Tea tree oil's antibacterial properties may help reduce inflammation.  Skin irritations from soaps, lotions, or fabrics can cause itchy skin. Chamomile, known for its calming effects, can help soothe irritated skin.  Some people may experience itchy skin due to internal issues like kidney problems or liver disease. Dandelion root, which stimulates digestion, may help with underlying issues.  In some cases, itchy skin can be a symptom of anemia, where the body lacks iron. Iron-rich herbs like nettle leaf may help alleviate anemia symptoms, including itchy skin.
